barRaise.topFinding: Twenty-sixth review, seventh straight healthy verdict. HEAD is still f61c179 (v0.51.3, 06-15), so the code board is byte-for-byte unchanged from the last six reviews: zero HIGH, two AI-owned MEDIUMs that neither block 1.0, and four LOWs. The only working-tree movement is the same cosmetic uncommitted edits (a CONTEXT.md size note in CLAUDE.md plus four comment-only stylesheet repoints in src/mobile-hooks*.ts). The lone item that moved is observability, and it moved the wrong way a seventh time: today clean 06-21 canonical audit landed but the dashboard audit.latestReportPath in Chains.json still points at CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-14, now seven audits stale (06-15 through 06-21 all clean on disk), up from six on 06-20. The gap widens by one every day the daily audit fires and no release does, because the dashboard refresh is welded to release time. It stays MEDIUM and benign only because all seven skipped audits are clean. Decoupling that refresh is the highest-value AI-ownable fix left before 1.0.

actions.title: Decouple the dashboard audit block from release time: have the daily canonical-audit writer parse the high/medium/low counts from the latest CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md TL;DR and advance audit.latestReportPath, so a between-release HIGH cannot sit invisible. The pointer is now seven audits stale (06-14 on the JSON, clean 06-15 through 06-21 on disk). Highest-value AI-ownable chore on the board.
Post-1.0, split the worker/src/index.js monolith into modules. No pre-1.0 action.
